system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{

    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;

    }
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.rhoSimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': "ERROR: 
wrong token type - expected word, found on line 47 the punctuation token '('

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/squareBend/2/system/blockMeshDict/edges at line 47.

    From function Foam::Istream& Foam::operator>>(Istream&, word&)
    in file primitives/strings/word/wordIO.C at line 74.

FOAM exiting"}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors:

### Error 1: Cannot Find Points File (Cascading)
This is purely a consequence of `blockMesh` failing — no mesh exists yet.

### Error 2: Wrong Token Type in blockMeshDict Edges (Persistent across 9+ attempts)

**Error Message:**
```
wrong token type - expected word, found on line 44 the punctuation token '('
```

Despite over 9 attempts, the edge parsing error persists. Every conceivable fix to the `edges` syntax has been tried. The fundamental problem is that the **OpenFOAM version being used does not accept the standard edge list format** with individual `(v1 v2)` entries separated by spaces.

## Root Cause

Looking at the error trace precisely: the parser encounters a `(` token where it expects a `word`. This happens because the `edges` sub-dictionary is **not recognized as a list/dictionary entry** — meaning the `edges` keyword itself may not be registered in this OpenFOAM version's parser, OR the file structure is malformed.

The real breakthrough came when examining the **exact format used in the reference squareBend tutorial**. The tutorial uses a **compact edge notation** where edges are listed as space-separated tuples WITHOUT the surrounding `edges (...)` wrapper, or the entire `edges` sub-dictionary is omitted entirely if no edges are needed.

## Proposed Fix — Remove the edges sub-dictionary entirely

Since the current mesh has relatively few edge splits and the primary goal is getting `blockMesh` to run, **remove the `edges` sub-dictionary completely** from `system/blockMeshDict`. OpenFOAM's `blockMesh` will still run and generate the mesh — the edge splits are optional refinement tools.

Delete the entire `edges` section:

```
blocks
(
    // Block 1: Channel from x=-50 to x=0
    hex (0 4 5 1 2 6 7 3)   (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Full-length block from x=0 to x=-500
    hex (4 8 9 5 6 10 11 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 3 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (12 13 15 14)
        );
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 6 2)
            (4 8 10 6)
            (8 12 14 10)
        );
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 5 7 3)
            (5 9 11 7)
            (9 13 15 11)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 2 6 4)
            (4 6 10 8)
            (8 10 14 12)
        );
    }
);
```

**Critical note about the outlet face:** The face `(12 13 15 14)` references vertices 12, 13, 14, 15 which are NOT part of any block. This is the **real root cause** that has persisted across all attempts. The outlet boundary face must reference faces that actually exist on the external surface of the blocks.

With only 2 blocks, the outlet-facing faces are:
- From Block 2: the face at x=-500 consisting of vertices (8,9,11,10) — i.e., `hex (8 9 11 10)`

So the **outlet boundary** should be:
```
outlet
{
    type patch;
    faces
    (
        (8 9 11 10)
    );
}
```

Similarly, the `upperWall` and `lowerWall` face definitions may need adjustment since Block 2 now spans the full length. The face `(8 12 14 10)` involves vertices 12 and 14 which are not in any block — those faces simply don't exist. The `upperWall` should consist of the top face of Block 2: `(8 10 14 6)` and the face `(4 8 10 6)` is internal. So:
```
upperWall
{
    type wall;
    faces
    (
        (4 8 10 6)
    );
}
```

And `lowerWall` similarly:
```
lowerWall
{
    type wall;
    faces
    (
        (5 9 11 7)
    );
}
```

The `frontAndBack` empty boundary should remain as `(0 2 6 4)` and `(4 6 10 8)` — these are valid faces from Block 1 and Block 2 respectively.

### Summary

| File | Issue | Action |
|------|-------|--------|
| `system/blockMeshDict` | **Primary**: The `edges` sub-dictionary triggers a parsing error in this OpenFOAM version | **Remove the entire `edges` section** — edge splits are optional and not required for basic mesh generation |
| `system/blockMeshDict` | **Secondary**: The `outlet` face `(12 13 15 14)` references non-existent vertices; `upperWall` and `lowerWall` reference non-existent vertices | Replace `outlet` with `(8 9 11 10)`, `upperWall` with `(4 8 10 6)`, `lowerWall` with `(5 9 11 7)` — only include faces that belong to the actual blocks |
| All other files | No issues (errors are cascading from blockMesh) | Will resolve once mesh is generated |

This approach discards the edge-based refinement (which is acceptable for initial validation) and focuses solely on generating a valid mesh topology that `blockMesh` can consume. The key insight from 9+ failed attempts is that the edge syntax keeps failing — removing it entirely bypasses this class of parser bugs.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace the entire edges sub-dictionary with nothing -- remove it entirely so blockMesh only processes the blocks and boundary sections; Replace outlet boundary face from (12 13 15 14) to (8 9 11 10) which are actual vertices on the outlet face of Block 2; Replace upperWall faces to only include (4 8 10 6) which is a valid face on Block 2; Replace lowerWall faces to only include (5 9 11 7) which is a valid face on Block 2; Keep frontAndBack empty faces unchanged as they correctly reference existing block faces'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
